storyInfo: Gordon Tannen's group is intercepted by a water clone. Behind the scenes, Mercia and Maylander agents sneak in and discover Rhine's secret. Nasti follows the trail of bone pen fragments to track down her foster mother, but then the ground cracks and a tree-like facility that Rhine has been hiding takes off into the night sky.

attributes.content: Where's the exit? According to the map, we should be able to see the checkpoint machines now...
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: A-Are we gonna keep going? There's nobody else here, and it's so eerily quiet...
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: Of course there's nobody else here, or we'd be exposed! Get your act together!
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Eek!
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: R-Red liquid... it's dripping from the ceiling! It fell on my face!
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: It's rusty water from the pipes... don't make me sew your mouth shut.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Mr. Tannen? You said we'd be exposed if there's anybody else here, right...
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: They look in the direction that Ferne is pointing, and see this 'somebody else'.
An ethereal figure walks towards them slowly from the end of the passage, flickering like a ghost in the dim light.
Something feels familiar...
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: Oh, shit...
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: Keep walking. My authority over the Rhine experiment area is legitimate and absolute. Doesn't matter who's coming—
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Is that so?
I would like an explanation, Tannen.
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: The Rhine experiment area is under investigation, Nasti. You agreed to cooperate with the administrative officers and the Propeller Paradise police.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: How am I supposed to cooperate with an investigation that I know nothing about?
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: Besides, it's news to me that guides are 'administrative officers' now.
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: The guide visited Rhine shortly before the lab accident. It makes perfect sense to have her along for evidence-gathering.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: As for the lack of prior notice, it's entirely within my rights to make the decision to ensure the integrity of the investigation...
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Gordon Tannen considered every potential contingency, even an interrogation by Gustave.

He knew he could not depend on Mercia, and he was confident he could handle this. But he cannot stop his thick tail from nervously slapping the ground, as though it senses a threat, something beyond his control.
Nasti is gazing at him, indecipherable, seemingly hiding something. Eeriness and anxiety cause Gordon Tannen to tighten his left fist in his pocket.
(I was just supposed to keep an eye out with a water clone hidden in the plants! Nasti never told me the head of Propeller Paradise would be so hard to fool!)
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: (I'd wear myself out arguing with him all night. Let's do this my way.)
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: Ahem!
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: The sound of dripping water, which accompanied the trio their whole way down the tunnel, is suddenly silent. Water droplets break free from the constraints of physics and float in midair.
They feel a coolness at their feet. Only now do they notice that the water on the floor has reached their ankles.
Mr. Tannen! Look! I-Is that still Director Lunorey?
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: Gordon Tannen cannot believe what he is seeing. Water streams into 'Nasti' from all directions, carrying her higher and higher, looking down at the three invaders.
Gordon Tannen has never seen such effects from an Arts unit. Prudence would certainly be in order, if this was Banshee witchcraft. But there is something that scares him even more...
'Nasti' bends down at him, with a cute expression that the one by the name would never wear.
There really isn't a single fresh thing on Terra, is there? I've heard this pre-written spiel a thousand times.
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: How about we talk about something else? Like, what's in those two big boxes of yours? Don't tell me they're presents for Rhine Lab? We've still got a ways to go until Christmas, I thought?
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: ...Who are you?
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Nasti, the director of Rhine's Engineering Section, whose Vivarium you just ruined a few hours ago. Who else?
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: She would never talk like... THAT. And why do you look like you're saluting? That's a casting gesture, isn't it?
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Heh, you noticed.
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: You can't fool me. I don't know what your Arts do, but this underground passage belongs to Rhine, and anything that happens will only come back to bite you.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: You're not satisfied with just losing the experiments for the exhibit, eh? You want to go to jail with the rest of your people?
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Let me remind you of something.
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: Like you said, this underground passage belongs to Rhine. And you personally gave the order to shut it off.
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: But I'd bet a whole lot of gold certificates that your people don't know about tonight's 'investigation'.
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: How many would know where to go to look for your corpses?
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: You dare...!
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: ...Run!
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: It is nighttime. Nasti feels a daze as she walks the streets of Propeller Paradise.
This place began as a proposal from Rhine, built by Nasti personally. But Nasti no longer recognizes any of the myriad inventions that adorn it.
She even has to stop and think in order to figure out how the inflatable street lamps by the roadside work.
Nasti cannot help but feel that she is walking into an unfamiliar life.
It's like when I first came to Rhine.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Muelsyse emerged from the sink, teasing me for my ignorance as she walked me through sterile lab cleaning procedures.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Nasti stops in her tracks. Another fragment of the bone pen lies quietly in the gaps of the bricks, having guided her all the way here.
She picks up the fragment, as she once did when she walked into a new life for the first time.
Young Nasti walks the forests of the Convallis, carrying a bag far larger than she is, and stuffing it with broken twigs.
It has been two weeks since she and Væla arrived in the Convallis, and Væla is busy with younger Banshees who come to her with questions of every kind.
Only when the Banshees are all asleep and there is no one in the valley, does Væla have time to take Nasti's hand and walk with her through this beautiful place...
Finally, Nasti decided to build a treehouse on her own. But the bag of twigs is too heavy for her, and she must wait for Væla to pick her up.
It has been so long since the rainy season. Isn't it time for another rowing competition? When do we...?
attributes.name: Young Banshee A

attributes.content: Let me clear the mud off the eastern shores of the lake.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: But no 'spin' incantation on the boat this time, please.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Teacher! One of the mistapple vines is getting in my house. Can I cut it?
attributes.name: Young Banshee B

attributes.content: No, my child. Nothing is better for wiping one's feather horns, than the pulp of the mistapple. Perhaps you could 'guide' the vine to grow in a different direction?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: It'll be a chance for me to see if you've been practicing the incantations I taught you.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: How long has it been since we last received a 'token' from outside, Custodian?
attributes.name: Young Banshee C

attributes.content: We've only had five or six this month. I dealt with them when I was in Kazdel to ask the Child of Soil and Stone to treat the Roosting Tree.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: So few?
attributes.name: Young Banshee C

attributes.content: Isn't it a good thing?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: It's been half a year since you taught me the elegies, but I haven't really had the chance to sing for anyone... can I go next time, and help our kin...?
attributes.name: Young Banshee C

attributes.content: Alright, let Væla rest.
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: It's all right. This is what I'm supposed to do.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: The children call you 'Teacher' and 'Custodian'. I never conferred these titles.
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: What you've done for the Convallis over the past two centuries puts me to shame as the Lord of the Banshees.
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: You know the reason. I have to keep working... to distract myself.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Did Duq'arael cause you any trouble during your audience with Her Highness? He's been hostile towards our people ever since we retired to the Convallis.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: He's hostile to everyone. But speaking of Her Highness...
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: She showed me the early fruits of her exploration into the nature of Originium. Perhaps she has already chosen a more resolute path.
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: What about us, then? What about the Convallis?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: The Convallis is no longer associated with the future of the Sarkaz, even if Her Highness really has changed her mind.
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: It remains the Banshee sanctuary.
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: You brought a girl back a little while ago, didn't you?
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: The daughter of a stray sister... I should have found her five years ago.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Aefanyl keeps asking me when he can come and see her... he's so curious about everything beyond the valley.
attributes.name: Laqeramaline

attributes.content: The boy...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Please tell Aefanyl that Nasti is rather timid and needs time to get used to the Convallis...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: That's not true. I'm not timid. You know that.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Yes. But you'll be living with other Banshees for a very long time.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: They'll share fruit with you, rather than throw rocks at you. Living with them will be very different from living in Kazdel.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: That's why I want your meeting to be more... formal. Give me time to prepare, won't you?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Understood.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: But what are you preparing? Does it have something to do with the 'token from outside' that you were talking about?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The things in your house? Those black blood crystals, dirty shrouds and dried-up hearts?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: But why do you say there were only five or six?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: You always stay up late, peering at them, with a deep frown on your face—
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: ...Nasti.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Do you like this valley?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Yes.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: There's no blood in the mud, no black rocks in houses, no fighting, plenty of food, and no one ever disturbs you...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Do you want to stay here?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Yes. That's why I want to build my own treehouse soon.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Have you decided where?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: There's a big, beautiful tree deep in the valley, right? What's the name... the Roosting Tree?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Yes. It's the Banshees' final resting place.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Everyone lives there, right? Even though you've never taken me there...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I want to live closer to that place. Can I?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: And when will you teach me incantations? That way, I can move this bag of twigs myself.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: No need to be so hurried.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: When will you teach me how to play the bone whistle? That way, I could play in front of the others when we play together.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I took one from your house, but I couldn't make a sound with it.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: No need to be so hurried, my dear girl.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: The tired Banshee suddenly seems so unfamiliar to Nasti. She knows where Væla always starts when polishing her own feather horns, and how many nearly indistinguishable black dresses she has.
But she can never tell what Væla is thinking.
Nasti stops asking questions and raises the bone whistle, obstinately blowing on it in futility.
Until a soaring melody covers up the muffled sound she is making.
You want to play the whistle for the other children, don't you?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: One does not need a bone whistle to accompany a smile.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Come, child. I'll teach you.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Nasti does not understand how that simple leaf in Væla's hands can make a sound, much less why that sound is so full of sorrow. The sky is blue, the waters of Mist Lake murmur on, and everything seems perfect.
Nasti follows Væla's gaze. The branches of the Roosting Tree are just barely visible in the distance, swaying like reeds—like fate.
Nasti is still in Rhine experiment area, and is preparing to kill Tannen?
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: Stay behind me, Miss Selene.
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: I run faster than Mr. Tannen, should we really get into a fight with a Banshee.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: But that 'Nasti' was just a potted plant.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: A water clone?
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: Muelsyse? Here?
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: Just a little gadget enchanted with Water Elf Arts. Doesn't actually do much.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: A combination of Nasti's image, Muelsyse's inspiration, and home field advantage... no one could have predicted it. No wonder those three were scared away.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: Should we evacuate with Mr. Tannen, then?
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: No. Keep going, following the traces left behind by the water.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: According to the blueprints, we're almost at a dead end.
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: There's nothing except the water marks. Maybe that was indeed a bluff by Muelsyse.
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: Nasti is a genius engineer. It wouldn't be hard for her to leave a hidden space out of the blueprints. Besides, the presence of the water clone itself is a sign that there are potential secrets here.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: I've been thinking. Why is Nasti the only one here, when there are two Rhine directors working on the project?
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: By all accounts, the Vivarium should fall within Muelsyse's domain, shouldn't it?
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: Unless... by its very nature, it's something only Nasti can do.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: You mean...
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: If your potted plant suddenly died—sorry, purely hypothetical—would your first reaction be to keep checking the time on your watch over and over?
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: No. I would be more concerned about the cause of death than the time.
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: But that was what the most experienced researchers near Nasti did when the incident at the Vivarium occurred—check the time.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: It was almost as though they expected the wilting to happen.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: It just didn't happen at the expected time.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: Therefore, Rhine's claim that they were studying plants that can survive at high altitude, based on data from the Galleria Stellaria...
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: It's a cover story.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: I think Maylander has found Rhine's true secret.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: Rhine doesn't need the plants in the Vivarium to live. It needs them to die.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: Not the life that an Elf celebrates, but the death that a Banshee knows well.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: But why?
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: Death will 'water'—
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: A 'tree' that is even more special.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: The elderly Banshee stands in the shadow at the end of the street. She has waited a long time. The night is longer than the three centuries that she has lived.
She watches as the familiar figure approaches...
It is taller and sturdier than she remembers.
The facial features look different. The clothes are of an unfamiliar style.
Not so familiar anymore.
Væla's head begins to ache. Her chaotic memories are like Mist Lake in deep winter, the cold and heavy mist swallowing her in an instant.
Na...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: (In Sarkaz) Separate.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: (In Sarkaz) Guide.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Nasti places the fragments of the bone pen in the center of her palm, and recites the language of the Banshees.
The incantation separates the scent that does not belong to her, but is very familiar, and leads her to its source.
There is no wind, but the bone pen begins to sway... all will be answered, soon.
Then—
Na... sti... careful...
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: What's the situation over there?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: There's... someone else...
attributes.name: Nasti?

attributes.content: *pant*... *pant*...
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: M-Mr. Tannen... stop...
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: I think we're lost.
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: I think we're lost too. I can't believe how much space Rhine has beneath the experiment area. This has got to be against the rules...
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Usually, when scientists leave so much space in their labs, they're either saving it for a big plan... or it's from a big plan that's already gone off the rails.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: S-Sir, is it just me, or is the ground...?
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: The ground's cracking...
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: ...Looks like it's the former.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: S-Somebody help...
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: When did the door behind us close?
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: Dammit, please tell me it's not a giant missile, Nasti.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: The concrete beneath their feet shatters, as though it were made from crackers.
Gordon Tannen tries to hook the pillars with his tail, grab the walls with his hands, but all he finds is... a single twig, glowing dimly.
Beneath him, a canopy made from metal and withered wood expands in the darkness.
The glowing tree rises up silently into the night sky, as if a thousand stars are rushing upwards.
This thing...
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Does it have a propeller? An engine?
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: There isn't any sound of machinery... how could it possibly fly?
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Nasti, Rhine Lab... I'll be completely damned.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Shit, why do these guards feel the need to shine lights into the sky just because some humongous thing is taking off in the middle of the night? Who the hell trained them—oh, right, it was me. Great.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Would you say we're... exposed, sir?
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: I would say we are, given that everyone who's awake in Propeller Paradise right now is seeing us hanging off a tree with two big boxes of contraband.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: You think there's a chance if I yelled that I found some dangerous Rhine experiments to the people below?
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: ...No, no, no. Not a finkin' chance. I can't let this thing get ruined, until I figure out how on Terra it could fly.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Okay, it really is over now.
attributes.name: Gordon

attributes.content: Wh-What just happened?
attributes.name: Maylander Agent

attributes.content: I think our intrusion might have awakened something...
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: But now, there are no secrets on Propeller Paradise anymore.
attributes.name: Mercia

attributes.content: Is this another miracle, Director Lunorey? Or is it...?
attributes.name: Sky Jagger

attributes.content: A stillborn miracle?
attributes.name: Sky Jagger

attributes.content: No need to say anything. I can see it all from here.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: ...I'll head back right away.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Na...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: A moment of hesitation, and the elderly Banshee fails to say the name.
Væla watches as the young Banshee departs. She looks in her direction, and sees a huge, glowing tree, slowly rising into the sky.
The roots of the tree are not secure, swaying in the night breeze. At this distance, they sway like weeds, like fate.
Is that... the Roosting Tree?
attributes.name: Væla
